name: Chicken and Smoked Sausage Gumbo
recipeIngredient: 1 pound smoked sausage, such as Andouille or Kielbasa, cut crosswise into 1/2-inch slices
1 pound boneless chicken meat, cut into 1-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large cast-iron or enameled cast-iron Dutch oven over medium heat, combine the oil and flour. Stir slowly and constantly for 20 to 25 minutes until a dark brown roux, the color of chocolate, forms.
2. Add the onions, celery, and bell peppers. Continue stirring for 4 to 5 minutes or until wilted.
3. Add the smoked sausage, salt, cayenne, and bay leaves. Stir for an additional 3 to 4 minutes.
4. Gradually add water, stirring until the roux mixture and water are well combined. Bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook, stirring occasionally, for 1 hour.
6. Season the chicken with the rub and add it to the pot. Simmer for an additional 2 hours. Skim off any fat that rises to the surface.
7. Remove from the heat. Stir in the parsley, green onions, and file powder.
8. Serve the gumbo in deep bowls, removing the bay leaves.
